content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 21, 1993

Phillip Giannangeli
Citizens Against Safety-Kleen
1627 Honey Hill Road
El Cajon, CA 92020

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-118

Dear Mr. Giannangeli:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Citizens Against
Safety-Kleen concerning the campaign provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act")¹

QUESTION

Does Citizens Against Safety-Kleen meet the Act's definition
of a committee?

CONCLUSION

Citizens Against Safety-Kleen does not meet the Act's defini-
tion of a committee.

FACTS

You are a member of an organization called the Citizens
Against Safety-Kleen. The purpose of the organization is to stop
the location of a hazardous waste facility. The organization col-
lects funds to pay for fliers, information sheets, voice mail,
postal box and room rental. The organization's objective is to
educate people so they will actively oppose the location of a
hazardous waste facility. The final decision on the location of
the facility will go before the city council.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Phillip Giannangeli
A-93-118
Page 2


ANALYSIS

     Section 82013(a) states that "committee" means any person or
combination persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year.  Section 82015
and Regulation 18215 provide that a contribution is any monetary
or nonmonetary payment made for political purposes for which full
and adequate consideration is not made to the donor.  A payment is
made for political purposes if it is:

     ...For the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence
the action of the voters for or against the nomination or
election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification
or passage of any measure.

                    Regulation 18215(a)(1).

     According to the facts presented in your letter, it appears
that the activities of Citizens Against Safety-Kleen have not
resulted in it qualifying as a committee under the Act.
Therefore, it is not required to file campaign disclosure state-
ments.

     Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

                              Sincerely,

                              Jeff Marschner
                              General Counsel

                              [signature]
                              By:  Sandra L. Silva
                                   Political Reform Consultant
                                   Technical Assistance Division

the action of the voters for or against the nomination or
